content.full_text:
January 4, 1990

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: CAL-HY-PAC

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

On behalf of our client CAL-HY-PAC, we are seeking advice on the campaign contribution provisions of the Political Reform Act.

Specifically, CAL-HY-PAC is the sponsored PAC of the California Dental Hygienists' Association. The PAC receives its contributions through a system where members of the association earmark a specific amount of their annual dues for purposes of funding the sponsored PAC. Most members, but not all, make an annual payment to the association of which a specified amount is earmarked for the PAC.

In most, if not all instances, the amount of the annual earmarked payment is less than $25. No individual member's total earmarked contributions from annual dues equals or exceeds $100.

Occasionally, members are solicited for direct contributions to the PAC above and beyond the normal amount earmarked through their dues payment.

What obligation is the PAC under for purposes of maintaining records on contributions of an individual member from annual dues payments? Specifically, is the PAC required to maintain records on association members whose annual dues earmarked for the PAC are less than

Jeanne Pritchard  
January 4, 1990  
Page 2

$25 if the PAC knows the cumulative total for the calendar year may on occasion exceed $25?

Thank you for your attention in this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO/jph
cc: Cal-Hy-Pac
